event: scheduled to undergo a left lobe hepatectomy
no medical problems except liver cirrhosis
liver cirrhosis (Child-Pugh class A) due to chronic hepatitis B
administered intravenous glycopyrrolate 0.2 mg as premedication
middle phase of the liver resection
sudden decrease in arterial blood pressure
resuscitation with the rapid intravenous administration of colloid and catecholamines
intraoperative ultrasonography revealed massive air emboli in both her left and right heart
systolic blood pressure and heart rate were maintained at approximately 90 mmHg and 110 beats/min
central venous pressure was approximately 2 mmHg
End-tidal carbon dioxide was restored to 32 mmHg
ABGA at 30 minutes after the occurrence of this episode
norepinephrine infusion (0.2-0.3 µg/kg/min) and fluid resuscitation was continued
colloid 1000 ml and crystalloid 500 ml was infused
air emboli in her left heart disappeared 70 min later
systolic pressure was maintained at approximately 90 mmHg
2,950 ml of fluid had been administered
systolic pressure was still maintained at approximately 90 mmHg
postoperative laboratory findings revealed an abnormal PT/PTT (25.5/51.8 sec)
postoperative EKG showed a ST elevation in II, III and aVF
trans-thoracic echocardiogram to evaluate her cardiac function
did not reveal the existence of patent foramen ovale (PFO)
at POD 5, her mental status had not changed
brain CT and MRI revealed multiple acute cerebral infarctions
at POD 11, she was weaned to spontaneous ventilation with CPAP mode and extubated
from POD 15, her vital signs became unstable
intravenous administration of catecholamines was started
finally, panperitonitis was confirmed by the identification of gram (+) cocci on the peritoneal culture
expired due to a cardiac arrest caused by septic shock
